title: CHAPTER 4 AFRICAN METHODIST EPISCOPAL CHURCH (INCORPORATION) ACT
text:
CHAPTER 4
AFRICAN METHODIST EPISCOPAL CHURCH
(INCORPORATION) ACT
• Act • Subsidiary Legislation •
ACT
Act No. 30 of 1974
ARRANGEMENT OF SECTIONS
1. Short title.
2. Incorporation of the Conference Board of Trustees.
3. Vesting of property.
4. Board of Trustees may acquire property.
5. Board of Trustees may dispose of property.
6. Mode of sealing of documents.
7. Service of documents on Board of Trustees.
CHAPTER 4
AFRICAN METHODIST EPISCOPAL CHURCH (INCORPORATION) ACT
An Act to provide for the incorporation of, and the vesting of property in, the
Incorporated Conference Board of Trustees of the African Methodist
Episcopal Church in Grenada.
[Act No. 30 of 1974.]
[11th April, 1975.]
1. Short title
This Act may be cited as the African Methodist Episcopal Church (Incorporation)
Act.
2. Incorporation of the Conference Board of Trustees
Reverend Flemming Emmanuel Joseph, Reverend Elder Walter Clarence, Reverend
Gwendolyn Onley, Mr. Wilson St. Bernard and Mr. Huntley Williams and their successors
in office as members of the Conference Board of Trustees of the African Methodist
Episcopal Church in Grenada (hereinafter called “the Church”) shall be a body
corporate known as The Incorporated Conference Board of Trustees of the African
Methodist Episcopal Church in Grenada (hereinafter called “the Board of Trustees”) and
by that name shall have perpetual succession and a Common Seal, and in that name
power to sue and be sued.
3. Vesting of property
All churches, chapels, mission houses, rectories, vicarages, parsonages, glebes,
buildings, and land vested in any person and appropriated to the uses of the Church
are transferred to and vested in the Board of Trustees.
4. Board of Trustees may acquire property
Subject to the rules, regulations and control of the Church in its Book of Discipline
the Board of Trustees shall have power to acquire for the Church by gift, grant,

purchase, devise or otherwise land, buildings, messuages and hereditaments of
whatsoever nature or kind in perpetuity or for life or lives or for a term of years, and
also money, goods and chattels, and subject to such rules, regulations and control and
to the performance of any trust upon which the said land, buildings, messuages,
hereditaments, money, goods and chattels may have been acquired, to take, hold and
enjoy the same.
5. Board of Trustees may dispose of property
Subject to the rules, regulations and control aforesaid and to the performance of
any such trust, the Board of Trustees may sell, demise, grant, convey, transfer,
alienate or otherwise dispose of any property vested by this Act in them or which may
hereafter be acquired by or become vested in them.
6. Mode of sealing of documents
All documents requiring the Common Seal of the Board of Trustees shall be sealed
in the presence of two members of the Board of Trustees who shall sign every such
document.
7. Service of documents on Board of Trustees
(1) The Board of Trustees shall at all times have a fixed address in Grenada for the
service of documents on the Board of Trustees and such address and any change
thereof shall be registered with the Registrar of Companies.
(2) Any document may be served on the Board of Trustees by leaving the
document at, or by sending it by registered post to, the address registered pursuant to
subsection (1).
CHAPTER 4
AFRICAN METHODIST EPISCOPAL CHURCH (INCORPORATION) ACT
SUBSIDIARY LEGISLATION
No Subsidiary Legislation
